hypothesis: If NaviDiv's adaptive, multi-faceted diversity constraints are effective during reinforcement learning-based molecular generation, then the generated set will exhibit higher diversity across representation, fragment, scaffold, and SMILES-based metrics while maintaining or improving the fraction of molecules that satisfy the singlet fission energy criterion ΔE_SF = E(S1) − 2·E(T1) within the target range, compared to baseline unconstrained generation.
